// StateCreating means bootstrap is in progress. Operations block until Ready.
// StateReady means bootstrap completed. All operations proceed normally.
// StateFailed means bootstrap failed. The error is stored in stateErr.
// Can transition back to StateCreating on retry via CreateWorkspaceForBootstrap.
// StateBootstrapFinished means bootstrap completed but re-bootstraps are
// pending (pendingRebootstraps > 0). Prompts block on this state (it's not

// WorkspaceLifecycle encapsulates workspace lifecycle state and a notification

// instead of logging a warning. Set to true in tests to catch bugs

// in StateFailed) under a single lock acquisition.
// transition is the internal helper that sets state, notifies waiters,
// records a metric with the time spent in the previous state, and logs
// a warning if the transition is not in validTransitions.
// transitionLocked performs a state transition while l.mu is already held.

// PushWithRebase does pull-rebase-push with retry. Upstream wins on conflicts.
func (mr *ManagedRepo) PushWithRebase(ctx context.Context, maxAttempts int) error {
// -X ours: during pull --rebase, "ours" = the remote branch being rebased onto.
// Upstream wins on conflict (remote content is preserved).

// not tracking-based push, so tracking config doesn't affect push behavior.
return fmt.Errorf("failed to rename origin to upstream (%s): %w", redactCredentials(stderr), err)
return fmt.Errorf("failed to add origin remote (%s): %w", redactCredentials(stderr), err)
